schedule_logic: start uniform gap8, add selected target indices as extra keyframes
encoder_allowed_inputs: deterministic RGB/motion proxy features from input frames
optional fixed RGB-only pretrained motion/feature networks in a higher-compute tier
decoder_allowed_inputs: transmitted keyframe schedule or keyframe indices
Stage158 residual payloads for non-keyframe middle recovery rows
normal StreamSplat endpoint inputs and normalized time for recovered middle rows
decoder_forbidden_inputs: target dense anchors except through encoded Stage158 residual payload
rendered quality metrics or oracle labels

rate_accounting_contract.counting_rule: count q-bit Gaussian anchor payload/metadata for every transmitted keyframe
rate_accounting_contract.notes: Use existing q12/qbit anchor accounting tables; if schedule changes keyframe count, anchor rate changes accordingly.

rate_accounting_contract.counting_rule: count schedule metadata for non-uniform selectors: keyframe count plus sorted indices or segment lengths packed with ceil(log2(total_frames)) bits/index
rate_accounting_contract.notes: Uniform fixed-gap schedules can be signaled by mode id only; adaptive schedules must transmit their indices or equivalent segment lengths.

rate_accounting_contract.counting_rule: count at least one small mode id when multiple schedule policies are available
count q6/keep1.0 entropy residual payload plus one-byte half selector for every recovered middle/intermediate frame
rate_accounting_contract.notes: Payload may vary by segment difficulty; Stage158 current policy is quality-first and not rate-minimized.

rate_accounting_contract.counting_rule: not counted as bitrate if features are not transmitted, but compute/dependency tier must be reported
rate_accounting_contract.notes: Pretrained optical flow or embeddings must be documented as higher-compute encoder tools.
